text: We raised $30 million from Sequoia, ATVC and ASTAR to build AI agents to learn how your
processes actually run and then run your operations.
Here's what makes Enterprise AI hard.
Your company's processes are specific, but the models are generic.
The way to bridge this gap is providing context in the form of a clear set of instructions
for the models to execute.
But do you have written instructions complete enough so I could run one of your company's
processes?
If you don't, you're missing what AI needs to be effective.
Even though you don't have them written, the data on how your processes are running today
is in your systems of record.
EDR reads this data, such as tickets, logs, emails, and reverse-engineers your processes,
policies and skills.
We then deploy agents equipped with your library of executable knowledge so they start automating
work, adhering to your specific processes.
With EDR, as your company evolves, so does your knowledge.
EDR continuously learns, spotting inconsistencies, process changes and gaps, bringing them to
your teams of experts to approve or dismiss.
Operators in a company go from just doing the work to also teaching your company's AI
agents.
Industry leaders like HubSpot, ASOS, Cushman and Wakefield and Avanti use EDR to run their
processes like IT service management, technical support, and more.
Today, we're coming out of stealth and we're opening pilots broadly.
The only thing we need to get started is a cut of your data and in one week we'll show
you your processes.
Clear, executable, and ready for agents.
